subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Ocean Worlds
description: Learn Ocean Worlds in one minute.

hook.text: Some exoplanets may be covered entirely by deep global oceans—water worlds with no land at all.

buildup.text: If a planet has enough water and the right temperature, oceans could be hundreds of kilometers deep.

discovery.text: K2-18b showed water vapor in its atmosphere in 2019. It may be a 'Hycean' world—a hydrogen-rich ocean planet.

twist.text: Deep enough oceans create exotic high-pressure ice at the bottom—not from cold, but from extreme pressure alone.

climax.text: JWST detected possible signs of dimethyl sulfide on K2-18b—a molecule made by life on Earth. The debate rages on.

punchline.text: Worlds of endless ocean. One might even carry a chemical hint of life.

quiz.question: What makes K2-18b a significant exoplanet discovery?
options: JWST detected possible biosignature molecules in its atmosphere
It is the closest ocean world to our solar system
It has a visible ocean surface photographed by telescopes
